events.name: Code Arena
events.description: A high-intensity competitive programming contest. Solve algorithmic challenges under time pressure across three escalating difficulty rounds. Only the sharpest coders survive.
rules: Languages allowed: C, C++, Java, Python
No internet access during the contest

events.name: Bug Bounty
events.description: Think you have an eagle eye for errors? Find and fix bugs hidden in code snippets across multiple languages. Speed and accuracy determine the champion debugger.
rules: No IDE or compiler—pen and paper only in Round 1

events.name: Paper Presentation
events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: AI/ML, IoT, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity
Abstract submission required before event day

events.name: Project Expo
events.description: Exhibit your innovative project with a working prototype or demo. Impress the judges with your creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.
rules: Must have a working demo or prototype
Project report must be submitted beforehand
Judging based on innovation, feasibility, and execution

events.name: IPL Auction
events.description: Step into the shoes of an IPL franchise owner! Teams get a virtual budget to bid for players in a live mock auction. Strategy, cricket knowledge, and quick thinking will crown the ultimate franchise builder.
rules: Each team gets a virtual budget of ₹80 Crores
No communication between teams during bidding

events.name: Meme War
events.description: Unleash your inner meme lord! Create the funniest memes based on given themes. The audience and judges will crown the ultimate Meme King/Queen.
rules: Memes must be original and created during the event
Audience voting counts for 40% of the score

events.name: Web Wizards
events.description: Design and develop a stunning web page from scratch within the given time. Participants will be judged on creativity, responsiveness, clean code, and visual appeal.
rules: Tech stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript only

events.name: Prompt Engineering
events.description: Master the art of crafting effective prompts for AI models. Compete to generate the best outputs using creative and precise prompt strategies across multiple rounds.
rules: 3 rounds: Basics, Creative Prompting, and Prompt Battle
Use of external tools is prohibited

events.name: Paper Presentation
events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: AI/ML, IoT, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity
Abstract submission required before event day

events.description: Exhibit your innovative project with a working prototype or demo. Impress the judges with your creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.
rules: Must have a working demo or prototype
Project report must be submitted beforehand
Judging based on innovation, feasibility, and execution

events.name: Treasure Hunt
events.description: Embark on a magical adventure across the campus! Solve riddles, decode clues, and race against other teams to find the hidden treasure.
rules: Clues will be a mix of tech-based and general riddles
All team members must stay together
No external help or phone usage
First team to find the treasure wins

events.name: Start Music
events.description: A thrilling music identification challenge! Listen to short clips and be the fastest to name the song. Covers Tamil, Hindi, and English tracks across eras.
rules: 3 rounds: Melody, Rapid Fire, and Finals
No use of phones or Shazam-like apps

events.name: AI Art Gallery
events.description: Use generative AI tools to create stunning digital artwork based on given themes. The best AI-artist collaboration wins! Explore the intersection of creativity and technology.
rules: Tools allowed: DALL·E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Leonardo AI
3 artworks must be generated in 45 minutes
Participants must explain their prompt strategy

events.name: Data Detective
events.description: Analyze a messy real-world dataset, uncover hidden insights, and present your findings. A thrilling challenge for aspiring data scientists and analysts.

events.name: Paper Presentation
events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: AI/ML, IoT, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity

events.name: Connexions
events.description: Guess the connection between seemingly unrelated images, words, or clues. A battle of lateral thinking and general knowledge that gets trickier with every round.
rules: Points awarded based on speed of guessing
Negative marking for wrong answers in final round

events.name: Dumb Charades
events.description: Act it out without a single word! A classic party game turned competitive—guess movies, songs, and tech terms through hilarious gestures and expressions.
rules: Categories: Movies, Songs, Tech Terms, Random
No speaking, lip-syncing, or pointing at objects

events.name: Electro Hunt
events.description: A clue-based answer hunt related to Electrical and Electronics.

events.name: Circuit Crafting
events.description: A hands-on circuit building challenge where teams race against time to construct and test basic electrical circuits using provided components.

events.name: Paper Presentation
events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: Power Systems, Renewable Energy, Smart Grid, Electric Vehicles or relavent to EEE.

events.name: Project Expo
events.description: Exhibit your innovative project with a working prototype or demo. Impress the judges with your creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.

events.name: Brain and Chalk
events.description: A pick one paper from the box and draw it on the board. give time limit of 1 min to draw and 1 min to guess. otherwise other teams can oppurtunity to guess the picture. if the picture is guessed correctly then the team gets points.
rules: Time limit: 1 min to draw and 1 min to guess
No speaking, lip-syncing, or pointing at objects

events.name: Beat the Clock 30
events.description: A clue-based answer hunt.

events.name: Robo Race
events.description: Build and race your robot through a challenging obstacle course! Navigate turns, ramps, and hurdles to reach the finish line first.

events.name: Sensor Challenge
events.description: Design innovative sensor-based solutions for real-world problems. Demonstrate your instrumentation skills by building smart measurement systems.

events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: IoT, Sensors, Automation, Robotics, Smart Systems

events.name: Ad-Mad Show
events.description: Create and perform a hilarious advertisement for a randomly assigned product. Creativity, humor, and presentation skills are key to winning this crowd favorite!

events.name: Musical Chairs Twist
events.description: The classic musical chairs with a twist! Each round comes with surprise challenges—dance, freeze, answer a question—before you can grab a chair.
rules: Must follow the twist challenge to sit
Multiple rounds until winner is decided

events.name: AutoCAD Sprint
events.description: Draft precision engineering drawings in AutoCAD under tight deadlines. Test your drafting skills with real-world component design challenges.

events.name: Engine Disassembly
events.description: Race to disassemble and reassemble a small engine correctly. Test your mechanical aptitude and understanding of engine components.
rules: Engine type: Single cylinder petrol engine

events.name: Paper Presentation
events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: EV Technology, IC Engines, Automotive Design, Hybrid Systems

events.name: Need for Speed
events.description: A thrilling RC car racing tournament! Navigate your remote-controlled car through a challenging track with obstacles and tight turns.
rules: Track has obstacles, ramps, and tight turns

events.name: Tug of War
events.description: Gather your squad and pull your way to victory! Pure strength, teamwork, and strategy in this classic showdown of power.

events.name: Bridge Builder
events.description: Design and construct a model bridge using popsicle sticks and glue. The bridge that holds the maximum load while meeting design specs wins!
rules: Materials: Popsicle sticks and fevicol only

events.name: Survey Master
events.description: Use total station and other surveying instruments to measure and map a given area. Precision and speed are the keys to victory.
rules: Must complete survey and submit report
Accuracy scored on a point system

events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.

events.name: Just a Minute (JAM)
events.description: Speak non-stop for one minute on a randomly given topic without hesitation, repetition, or deviation. A test of fluency and wit!

events.name: Short Film Festival
events.description: Lights, Camera, Action! Submit your original short film (under 7 minutes) on any socially relevant or creative theme. Best film wins the Golden Reel Award.
rules: Must be original content; no copyrighted music
Submit via Google Drive link before the event
Films will be screened and judged live

events.name: PCB Design Challenge
events.description: Design a PCB layout for a given circuit schematic using EDA tools. Clean routing, signal integrity, and component placement will be judged.

events.name: Signal Decode
events.description: Analyze and decode signals using oscilloscopes and signal analyzers. A hands-on challenge testing your communication engineering fundamentals.
rules: Each puzzle has a time limit

events.name: Gaming Arena – BGMI
events.description: Survive, loot, and conquer in the ultimate BGMI tournament! Squad up and fight for the chicken dinner and bragging rights.
rules: Players must bring their own mobile devices
3 matches – points based on kills and placement

events.name: Gaming Arena – Valorant
events.description: Assemble your squad and dominate the battlefield in a 5v5 Valorant tournament! Show off your tactics, aim, and teamwork.
rules: Map pool: Bind, Haven, Ascent, Split
Players must bring their own peripherals

events.name: CAD Warfare
events.description: Model complex 3D components using SolidWorks or CATIA in a speed challenge. Precision engineering meets digital craftsmanship.

events.name: Shark Tank
events.description: Pitch your startup idea to a panel of industry mentors and faculty 'sharks'. Convince them to invest in your vision!
rules: Business model canvas must be submitted beforehand
Feasibility and innovation are key judging criteria

events.name: Arm Wrestling
events.description: Show your raw strength in this classic one-on-one showdown! Multiple weight categories ensure fair competition for everyone.
rules: Both hands must stay on the table
